topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The afternoon sun warmed Elias's skin, a pleasant, heavy blanket draped over him as he lounged on the porch swing. A half-finished novel lay open in his lap, the pages slightly creased from his resting posture. He took a slow sip of his iced tea, the condensation cool against his fingertips. His breathing was deep and even, the sounds of distant lawnmowers providing a gentle, rhythmic backdrop to his stillness. Then, his phone buzzed. A notification flashed across the screen: a friend request. Elias blinked once, then looked down at the notification. A faint, almost imperceptible tightening around his jaw was the only outward sign of his attention. He considered ignoring it, letting the digital intrusion simply fade into the background noise of his peaceful afternoon.

[story 2]

Maya was sprawled on the oversized beanbag chair, a thick novel resting on her chest, the world outside the window reduced to muted greens and browns. She was completely at ease, her limbs loosely draped, a faint smile playing on her lips as she absorbed the quiet hum of the apartment. Her phone chimed insistently. It was a request from 'Roxanne_B.' Maya let the phone rest on the cushion beside her, not immediately reaching for it. A slow, almost lazy stretch pulled at her shoulders, and she glanced at the screen. A flicker of something—a brief, almost involuntary tightening in her chest—crossed her features, quickly smoothed over by a deliberate sigh. She didn't tap the notification; she simply observed it, the gesture of waiting radiating from her.

[story 3]

For Liam, the evening had settled into a comfortable inertia. He was curled up on the sofa, a worn hoodie pulled over his head, the ambient lighting from a nearby lamp casting soft shadows across the room. He was completely unhurried, his gaze drifting aimlessly across the ceiling. His fingers drummed a soft, unconscious rhythm on the armrest. When his phone vibrated, he didn't immediately pick it up. He let the vibration sit there for a moment, a tiny disruption in the quietude. When he finally looked down, his expression remained placid, almost blank, until he registered the profile picture. A subtle stiffening entered his posture, a slight drawing inward of his shoulders, as he processed the unexpected contact.
